story: After a careful flirt by Ye Old Guard, Arrow and I publicly insist we’re not in love. Next morning my parents press about Jack; I see fear is letting him keep stealing from me and choose to risk love: I square my shoulders and say—Arrow and I are dating. That night, by a stubborn campfire I try to light alone. Arrow appears, offers help. I tell him I must start this fire myself. He nods, says he hopes there’s a fire he can help tend someday. I meet his eyes: “Perhaps.”
implausible_knowledge: I square my shoulders and say—Arrow and I are dating.
question: When Arrow finds her at the campfire that night and she insists on starting it herself, what will he most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask her out plainly and stay to plan their first date.
answer_without_knowledge: Respect her boundary, wish her good night, and leave her to tend the fire alone.
